Spider-Man 3 (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $338,007,351Details
International Box Office $558,329,917
Worldwide Box Office $896,337,268
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $126,623,033
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $910,386
Total $127,533,419
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Peter Parker and M.J. seem to finally be on the right track in their complicated relationship, but trouble looms for the superhero and his lover. Peter’s Spider-Man suit turns black and takes control of him, not only giving Peter enhanced power but also bringing out the dark side of his personality. Peter must overcome the suit’s influence as two supervillains, Sandman and Venom, rise up to destroy him and all those he holds dear.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$151,116,516 (44.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.24 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:37.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$258,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,252 opening theaters/4,324 max. theaters, 6.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $586,553,835

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 4th, 2007 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
May 4th, 2007 (IMAX) by Sony Pictures
April 29th, 2024 (Limited) by Sony Pictures
September 28th, 2025 (Special Engagement) by Fathom Events
International Releases: May 3rd, 2007 (Wide) (Australia)
May 3rd, 2007 (Wide) (New Zealand)
May 4th, 2007 (Wide), released as Örümcek-Adam 3 (Turkey)
June 20th, 2024 (Limited) (Re-release) (Netherlands)
August 16th, 2024 (Special Engagement) (2024 Re-issue, one week only) (United Kingdom)
Video Release: October 30th, 2007 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
October 15th, 2007 by Sony Pictures
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of intense action violence
Running Time: 139 minutes
Franchise: Spider-Man
Character Appearance: Spider-Man
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: College Professor, Death of a Best Friend, Escape, Faulty Memory, Hostage, Revenge
Social setting: College, Nightclub
Source material: Marvel Comics
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 2000s
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Marvel Studios, Laura Ziskin Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Peter Parker and M.J. seem to finally be on the right track in their complicated relationship, but trouble looms for the superhero and his lover. Peter’s Spider-Man suit turns black and takes control of him, not only giving Peter enhanced power but also bringing out the dark side of his personality. Peter must overcome the suit’s influence as two supervillains, Sandman and Venom, rise up to destroy him and all those he holds dear.
